highlights.text: Given the magnitude of this Failed Breakdown, I was looking for this to continue into this week, stating my target on Friday at 4pm: “ My lean is we work up it to 6732, 6744, then 6766/83.” This could not worked out better and right off the open Sunday we began the trek upwards, hitting 6783 by yesterday afternoon to set the high of day.
As I wrote yesterday: “In a parabolic bull case though, ES won’t even dip that low.

Readers know the answer to this: ES follows a standardized cycle where it alternates between elevator down sells (these are vicious drops lower that knife every support), and short squeezes (this is where ES snaps back the other way with equal or more velocity).
The squeezes are caused by Failed Breakdowns.
Early Friday morning, ES went elevator down selling 90 points rapidly down to 6573.
I wrote on Thursday at 4pm: “Obvious setup here the recovery of Tuesday’s 6593 daily low.” We held 6572, recovered 6593 early Friday morning, then ripped all day.
Failed breakdowns tend to magnetize towards major pattern resistances, and we saw this yesterday.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive review into this Friday Failed Breakdown that gave us the monster 190+ point Monday rally.

post_title: After a 200 Point Squeeze, SPX Rested and Coiled Today. Another Big Move Coming? Oct 22 Plan
